title: Middle East war unleashes a new economic shock
subhed: Since March 2, gas prices in Europe have increased by 50% and oil by 12%, while the Paris stock market has fallen by 4.7%. The economic impact depends on how long the conflict will last, but stagflation is likely. Asia and Europe will be hit hardest, while the US is somewhat more protected.
byline: By Eric Albert and Béatrice Madeline

title: After Iranian drone attack, Azerbaijan says 'preparing retaliatory measures'
subhed: At least two people were wounded after Iranian drone strikes on an airport and near a school in Azerbaijan on Thursday, according to a statement by the foreign ministry. Iran has denied launching the attack and blamed it on Israel.

title: Israel targets Iran's internal security establishment to facilitate postwar uprising
subhed: Israeli air forces dominate the skies above Tehran, targeting Iran's political institutions and its regime's security establishment in an effort to trigger its collapse.
byline: By Ghazal Golshiri and Louis Imbert

title: With the Strait of Hormuz blocked, regional trade disruptions could ripple across the globe
subhed: Following the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, maritime traffic in the Persian Gulf has ground to a halt. These disruptions risk causing congestion in Asian ports.
byline: By Julien Bouissou and Marc Angrand

title: Early days of war against Iran boost satisfaction in Israel
subhed: Israel has continued to bombard Iran with massive, unrelenting waves of military aircraft, while also attacking Hezbollah in Lebanon. Its missile defense system has helped limit the damage on Israeli territory.

title: How Iran secretly acquired facial recognition technology through a Russian company
subhed: An investigation coordinated by Forbidden Stories, in partnership with Le Monde, reveals that an Iranian company purchased and then resold the Russian surveillance software FindFace to various entities within the regime.

title: In the West Bank's Qaryout, a Palestinian village is besieged by settlers
subhed: Two brothers were killed during an attack by Israeli settlers. As war with Iran rages, settlement expansion in the West Bank is continuing at an unprecedented pace, with almost total impunity.

title: Iran's neighbors, under drone attacks, confront their vulnerability
subhed: Lacking experience in countering kamikaze drones, especially the infamous Shahed models, the Gulf countries have shown how unprepared they were for the threat posed by Iran, which is determined to exploit their vulnerability.
byline: By Chloé Hoorman and Marie Jégo
